description: This figure presents microstructural characterization of the M1-IA sample, which is an Si-free low-density medium Mn steel (Fe-8Mn-3Al-0.2C) subjected to intercritical annealing at 700 °C for 1 h followed by water quenching after hot rolling. Subfigures a₁ to a₄ are secondary electron SEM images at different magnifications with scales of 20 μm, 5 μm, 3 μm, 2 μm respectively, labeling α-ferrite and martensite-austenite (α'+γ) constituent phases. Subfigure b is an EBSD phase map with a 5 μm scale, where orange areas represent austenite and white areas correspond to ferrite. The annealed M1 sample has a dual-phase microstructure of α-ferrite and retained austenite with 45 vol% austenite. SEM images show the morphology of ferrite matrix and dispersed austenite/martensite-austenite islands, while the EBSD map intuitively presents the spatial distribution of austenite. This figure confirms that intercritical annealing produces a multiphase microstructure with high austenite content in M1 steel, supporting that high retained austenite enables excellent TRIP effect and superior mechanical properties.
